title: Why Attention Beat RNNs
description: An ML curious PM asks how Transformers work and why they replaced RNNs.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Everyone keeps saying Transformers won. Won what, exactly?

templates.text: Sequence modeling. They replaced RNNs and LSTMs as the default for text, audio, and now even images.
templates.hint_zh: 序列建模。它們取代了 RNN 跟 LSTM 成為文字、音訊、甚至影像的預設架構。

templates.text: They process one step at a time, so you can't parallelize across the sequence. And long-range info gets lost on the way.
templates.hint_zh: 它們一次處理一步,沒辦法在序列方向平行;長距離訊息在傳遞中會逐漸流失。

templates.text: Yes. Every position can look at every other position directly. That's full parallelism plus direct long-range links.
templates.hint_zh: 對。每個位置都能直接看其他所有位置,既可平行,長距離又有直接通道。

templates.text: Compute scales quadratically with sequence length. That's why context windows are still expensive.
templates.hint_zh: 計算量隨序列長度平方成長,所以長 context 還是貴。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: And multi-head attention? Same idea or different?

templates.text: Same idea, run in parallel across different subspaces. Each head can focus on a different relationship.
templates.hint_zh: 同樣的想法,在不同子空間平行跑。每個 head 可以關注不同的關係。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. So when I read a vendor pitch claiming 'attention-based', it's basically table stakes now.

templates.text: Pretty much. The interesting question is what they do on top.
templates.hint_zh: 差不多。真正值得問的是他們在這上面做了什麼。
